```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  mu <- sample(seq(-3,5,0.5),1); a <- sample(c(-3,-2,2,3,4),1); b <- sample(-5:5,1)
  ans <- a*mu+b; vals <- round(c(ans,a*mu,a*(mu+b),mu+b,a^2*mu+b),3)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Se $E(X)=`r mu`$, qual é $E(`r a`X+`r b`)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A linearidade fornece
$$E(aX+b)=aE(X)+b=(`r a`)(`r mu`)+`r b`
=`r sprintf("%.3f",ans)`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S8Q4_linearidade_esperanca
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
